title: T-043 — IndexedDB sync queue primitive (idb-keyval wrapper for offline mutations)
prompt:
# T-043 — IndexedDB sync queue primitive (idb-keyval wrapper for offline mutations)

PRD: `00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5 frontend (IndexedDB sync queue), §10 (Idempotent mutations [R14])
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`

## Goal
Client-side IndexedDB queue primitive that buffers scanner-originated mutations when offline. Mutations carry a client-generated transaction_id (UUID v7) so the server can deduplicate replays per §10 R14. The flusher endpoint is T-044.

## Implementation contract
(1) Add packages/sync-queue workspace package using idb-keyval as the IndexedDB wrapper. (2) Export enqueue(mutation: { transaction_id: string, endpoint: string, method: 'POST'|'PUT', body: unknown, created_at: string }) → Promise<void>; mutations stored under key `mut:${transaction_id}`. (3) Export listPending() → Promise<Mutation[]> ordered by created_at ASC. (4) Export remove(transaction_id: string) → Promise<void>. (5) Export generateTransactionId() → UUID v7 string (use uuid v9 lib). (6) Vitest with fake-indexeddb covers enqueue→list→remove + idempotent enqueue (re-enqueue same transaction_id is no-op).

## Files (scope)
- packages/sync-queue/package.json [create]
- packages/sync-queue/src/index.ts [create]
- packages/sync-queue/src/__tests__/queue.test.ts [create]
- packages/sync-queue/tsconfig.json [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given an empty queue, when enqueue is called with transaction_id='01HXY…' + endpoint='/api/scan/move' + method='POST' + body, then listPending() returns exactly 1 entry whose transaction_id matches
2. Given enqueue is called twice with the same transaction_id, when listPending() runs, then it returns exactly 1 entry (idempotent — R14 contract)
3. Given 3 entries exist, when remove(t2.transaction_id) is called, then listPending() returns 2 entries excluding t2 in the original order
4. Given generateTransactionId() is called 1000 times, when the results are sorted lexicographically, then the order matches insertion order (UUID v7 time-ordered property)

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/sync-queue/src/__tests__/queue.test.ts with fake-indexeddb covering all four ACs before src/index.ts exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/sync-queue test'

## Risk red lines
- Do not implement the flusher / network call here — that is T-044 (server-side endpoint + retry policy)
- Do not use localStorage as a fallback — IndexedDB is mandatory per PRD §5
- Do not generate UUID v4 — must be v7 for time-ordering (R14 idempotency depends on monotonic order)

## Dependencies (must be complete first)
- T-001

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED test before implementation) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Client-side IndexedDB queue primitive (enqueue / list / remove / UUID v7 generator) for offline-buffered scanner mutations.
